text:
Sleep deprivation ________ a range of serious health problems.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Is associated with' links sleep deprivation to health problems — it indicates a known connection or correlation between two things.
wrongReasons: 'In contrast to' introduces a comparison showing difference — it cannot connect a cause to its effects.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — it introduces a condition that is ignored, not a consequence.
'In order to' expresses purpose — sleep deprivation is not a goal, so this phrase makes no sense here.
hebrewExplanation: is associated with = קשור ל / מלווה ב. שימוש: מתאר קשר או מתאם בין שני דברים, נפוץ במחקר מדעי. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'leads to' — associated with מתאר קשר, לא בהכרח סיבתיות.

text:
The study was ________ previous research conducted in the 1990s.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Based on' shows that the new study used earlier research as its foundation — it is grounded in or derived from that prior work.
wrongReasons: 'In contrast to' shows opposition or difference — this contradicts the idea of building on prior work.
'As a result of' expresses a consequence — the study itself is not a consequence of the earlier research; it builds upon it.
'Is likely to' expresses probability — it is a verb phrase that cannot function as a complement here.
hebrewExplanation: based on = מבוסס על. שימוש: מציין את הבסיס או המקור של משהו. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'as a result of' — based on מתאר בסיס, לא תוצאה.

text:
________ the professor, the exam will cover all chapters from the textbook.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'According to' introduces a source or authority — here it attributes the information about the exam to the professor.
wrongReasons: 'In addition to' means 'besides' or 'also' — it adds information and cannot introduce a source.
'In terms of' frames a topic or perspective — it cannot attribute a statement to a person.
'Owing to' means 'because of' — it introduces a cause, not a source of information.
hebrewExplanation: according to = לפי / על פי. שימוש: מציין מקור מידע — אדם, מחקר, או טקסט. טעות נפוצה: להשתמש ב-'according to me' — בעברית אומרים 'לדעתי' אך באנגלית אקדמית יש להשתמש ב-'in my opinion'.

text:
The new policy was introduced ________ reduce traffic congestion in the city center.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'In order to' expresses purpose — the policy was introduced with the goal or aim of reducing congestion.
wrongReasons: 'As a result of' expresses consequence — it would mean congestion caused the policy, not that the policy aims to fix it.
'In spite of' introduces concession — it suggests the policy was introduced despite reducing congestion, which is illogical.
'Apart from' means 'besides' or 'except for' — it cannot introduce a purpose or goal.
hebrewExplanation: in order to = על מנת ל / כדי ל. שימוש: מביע מטרה — הפעולה נעשית למען השגת מטרה מסוימת. טעות נפוצה: לשכוח שאחרי in order to בא פועל בצורת הבסיס (infinitive).

text:
The Mediterranean diet ________ a healthy lifestyle and longevity.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Contributes to' means the diet plays a part in producing a healthy lifestyle — it adds to or supports something positive.
wrongReasons: 'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — it expresses indifference to a condition, not a connection.
'In accordance with' means 'in agreement with' or 'following rules' — a diet cannot be in accordance with longevity.
'In contrast to' shows opposition — the diet does not oppose a healthy lifestyle; it supports one.
hebrewExplanation: contributes to = תורם ל / מסייע ל. שימוש: מתאר תרומה חיובית לדבר מה. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'leads to' — contributes to מרמז על תרומה חלקית, leads to מרמז על תוצאה ישירה.

text:
The ancient ruins ________ the most impressive archaeological site in the region.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Are regarded as' expresses how something is perceived or classified by others — the ruins are considered to be the most impressive site.
wrongReasons: 'Owing to' means 'because of' — it introduces a cause, not a classification or perception.
'In terms of' frames a topic — 'The ruins in terms of the most impressive site' is grammatically incomplete and illogical.
'Have been shown to' is followed by a verb — 'the most impressive archaeological site' is a noun phrase, not an action.
hebrewExplanation: is regarded as = נחשב ל / נתפס כ. שימוש: מביע כיצד משהו נתפס על ידי אחרים או בתחום מסוים. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is defined as' — regarded as מתאר תפיסה, defined as מתאר הגדרה פורמלית.

text:
Poverty ________ higher crime rates in urban areas, researchers have found.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Is linked to' indicates a connection or relationship between two phenomena — researchers have found a link between poverty and crime rates.
wrongReasons: 'In order to' expresses purpose — poverty is not a goal; it does not aim to increase crime rates.
'In addition to' means 'besides' — it adds one thing to another and cannot express a causal or correlational relationship.
'In accordance with' means 'following' or 'consistent with rules' — poverty cannot follow or comply with crime rates.
hebrewExplanation: is linked to = קשור ל / מקושר ל. שימוש: מתאר קשר בין שני גורמים, נפוץ בדיווחי מחקר. טעות נפוצה: לחשוב שזה מרמז על סיבתיות — linked to רק מתאר קשר, לא הוכחת סיבה.

text:
________ its high cost, the treatment has become the standard approach in most hospitals.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'In spite of' introduces a concession — the treatment became standard despite the obstacle of its high cost.
wrongReasons: 'According to' introduces a source of information — 'according to its high cost' is meaningless; cost is not a source.
'As a result of' introduces a cause — saying the treatment became standard because of its high cost is illogical.
'In order to' expresses purpose — 'in order to its high cost' is ungrammatical and illogical.
hebrewExplanation: in spite of = למרות. שימוש: מביע ניגוד — משהו קורה למרות מכשול. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'because of' — in spite of מביע ניגוד, לא סיבה.

text:
The results of the clinical trial ________ those of earlier studies conducted in Europe.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Are consistent with' means the results agree with or match those of earlier studies — a standard academic expression for alignment between findings.
wrongReasons: 'Are grounded in' means 'are based on' — the results are not built upon the earlier studies; they simply agree with them.
'Give rise to' means 'cause' or 'produce' — the results do not produce earlier studies; this reverses the logic.
'Tend to' expresses a general habit or inclination followed by a verb — 'tend to those of earlier studies' is ungrammatical.
hebrewExplanation: are consistent with = עקביים עם / תואמים. שימוש: מתאר התאמה בין ממצאים שונים, נפוץ מאוד בכתיבה אקדמית מדעית. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'are based on' — consistent with מתאר הסכמה, לא מקור.

text:
The program was designed ________ providing students with practical skills for the job market.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'With the aim of' expresses purpose — the program was designed with the goal of providing practical skills.
wrongReasons: 'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — the program was not designed while ignoring practical skills; that is the goal.
'In contrast to' shows opposition — the program does not oppose practical skills; it aims to provide them.
'As opposed to' introduces a contrast — it would mean the program was designed as a contrast to practical skills, which is the opposite of the meaning.
hebrewExplanation: with the aim of = במטרה ל / מתוך מטרה ל. שימוש: מביע מטרה, נפוץ בכתיבה אקדמית ופורמלית. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'in order to' — שניהם מבטאים מטרה, אך with the aim of עוקב אחריו שם עצם/פועל בגרונד, בעוד in order to עוקב אחריו פועל בצורת הבסיס.

text:
The coastal regions ________ frequent flooding, which threatens both infrastructure and residents.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Are characterized by' describes a defining feature of the coastal regions — frequent flooding is a key characteristic of these areas.
wrongReasons: 'Play a key role in' means they contribute to something — 'coastal regions play a key role in frequent flooding' reverses the relationship illogically.
'Are derived from' means 'are obtained from' or 'originate from' — coastal regions do not originate from flooding.
'Have been shown to' is followed by a base verb — 'have been shown to frequent flooding' is ungrammatical.
hebrewExplanation: is characterized by = מאופיין ב / מוגדר על ידי. שימוש: מתאר תכונה בולטת או מאפיין ייחודי של נושא. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is defined by' — characterized by מתאר תכונות כלליות, defined by מתאר הגדרה מדויקת.

text:
Children who grow up bilingual ________ perform better on tasks requiring attention and cognitive flexibility.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Tend to' expresses a general inclination or pattern — bilingual children generally perform better, though not always.
wrongReasons: 'In terms of' frames a topic — 'children in terms of perform better' is ungrammatical; 'in terms of' must be followed by a noun phrase.
'Apart from' means 'besides' or 'except for' — it introduces an exception, not a habitual pattern.
'In accordance with' means 'in compliance with rules' — bilingual children do not perform better by following rules.
hebrewExplanation: tends to = נוטה ל / בדרך כלל. שימוש: מתאר נטייה כללית — לא תמיד, אך לרוב. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is likely to' — tends to מדבר על הרגל/נטייה, is likely to מדבר על הסתברות במקרה ספציפי.

text:
The findings suggest that regular physical activity ________ reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Plays a key role in' indicates that physical activity is an important contributing factor in reducing cardiovascular risk — it has a significant function in this process.
wrongReasons: 'Is inclined to' describes a tendency of a living being and requires a verb infinitive — 'inclined to reducing' is ungrammatical.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — physical activity does not ignore cardiovascular risk; it reduces it.
'Is derived from' means 'comes from' or 'originates in' — physical activity is not derived from reducing risk; this reverses the logic.
hebrewExplanation: plays a key role in = ממלא תפקיד מפתח ב / חיוני ל. שימוש: מתאר תרומה חשובה, חזק יותר מ-'plays a role in'. טעות נפוצה: להשתמש ב-'plays a key role to' — המילה הנכונה אחריה היא 'in'.

text:
The country's economic development has lagged, ________ its abundant natural resources.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Despite' (equivalent to 'in spite of') introduces a concession — the country lags economically even though it has abundant resources, which is unexpected.
wrongReasons: 'As a result of' expresses a cause — saying economic development has lagged because of abundant resources implies resources cause the lag, which distorts the meaning.
'In accordance with' means 'following rules or standards' — lagging cannot be in accordance with resources.
'Contributes to' must function as a verb phrase with a subject — 'the country's development contributes to its resources' is grammatically possible but changes the meaning entirely.
hebrewExplanation: despite / in spite of = למרות. שימוש: מביע ניגוד — תוצאה בלתי צפויה נוכח נסיבות מסוימות. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'despite of' — הצורה הנכונה היא 'despite' בלבד (ללא 'of'), בניגוד ל-'in spite of'.

text:
The new policy applies to all employees, ________ their years of experience or seniority.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — the policy applies to everyone, no matter how much experience they have.
wrongReasons: 'In addition to' means 'besides' — 'the policy applies to all employees in addition to their years of experience' would mean it also applies to the experience itself, which is illogical.
'Owing to' means 'because of' — saying the policy applies because of experience implies only experienced employees are covered, contradicting 'all employees'.
'Is based on' is a verb phrase — it cannot function as a connector between 'all employees' and 'their years of experience' in this sentence structure.
hebrewExplanation: regardless of = ללא קשר ל / בלי התחשבות ב. שימוש: מדגיש שהכלל או העובדה חלים בכל מצב, ללא יוצא מן הכלל. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'irrespective of' — שניהם נכונים ומשמעותם זהה.

text:
The rise in global temperatures ________ the melting of polar ice caps, which has alarmed scientists worldwide.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Has given rise to' means 'has caused' or 'has produced' — the rise in temperatures has led to or produced the melting of ice caps.
wrongReasons: 'Is regarded as' expresses perception — 'the rise in temperatures is regarded as the melting of ice caps' incorrectly equates the two things.
'In terms of' frames a topic — it must be followed by a noun phrase and cannot serve as a verb connecting cause and effect.
'Is inclined to' expresses a personal tendency and requires a verb — temperature rise cannot be 'inclined to' melt ice caps.
hebrewExplanation: give rise to = גרם ל / הוביל ל / הצמיח. שימוש: מתאר שמשהו גרם להופעתו של משהו אחר, נפוץ בהקשרים אקדמיים. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'leads to' — give rise to מרמז לרוב על תוצאה שהתפתחה בהדרגה.

text:
Research ________ that meditation significantly reduces stress levels among university students.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Has been found' is a standard academic expression meaning research has discovered or determined something through investigation.
wrongReasons: 'Has been shown' requires a subject that was demonstrated — 'research has been shown that' is grammatically awkward; 'research has shown that' or 'it has been shown that' would be correct.
'Is characterized by' describes a defining feature — 'research is characterized by that meditation reduces stress' is grammatically and logically incorrect.
'Plays a role in' describes a contribution — research does not play a role in the students' stress; research discovers things.
hebrewExplanation: has been found (that) = נמצא כי / התגלה כי. שימוש: מדווח על ממצא מחקרי בגוף סביל. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'has been shown to' — has been found that עוקב אחריו משפט שלם, has been shown to עוקב אחריו פועל.

text:
________ the traditional approach, the new method focuses on student collaboration rather than individual memorization.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'As opposed to' introduces a contrast between two approaches — the new method is different from and opposed to the traditional one.
wrongReasons: 'As a result of' expresses causation — saying the new method focuses on collaboration because of the traditional approach does not make logical sense here.
'In addition to' means 'besides' — this would imply both approaches are used together, but the sentence contrasts them.
'In accordance with' means 'following the rules of' — the new method does not follow the traditional approach; it opposes it.
hebrewExplanation: as opposed to = בניגוד ל / לעומת. שימוש: מציג ניגוד בין שני דברים, נפוץ בהשוואות אקדמיות. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'as well as' — as opposed to מביע ניגוד, as well as מביע הוספה.

text:
The educational system ________ standardized testing as its primary method of evaluation.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Is reliant upon' means 'depends on' — the educational system depends on standardized testing as its main evaluation tool.
wrongReasons: 'Gives rise to' means 'causes' — the system does not produce standardized testing in this context; it uses it.
'Is likely to' expresses probability and requires a following verb — 'is likely to standardized testing' is ungrammatical.
'Has been shown to' reports a finding and requires a following verb — 'has been shown to standardized testing' is ungrammatical.
hebrewExplanation: is reliant upon / relies on = תלוי ב / מסתמך על. שימוש: מתאר תלות או הסתמכות על כלי, שיטה, או גורם. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'is reliant on' — שתי הצורות (on/upon) נכונות.

text:
The theory ________ the assumption that human behavior is primarily driven by self-interest.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Is grounded in' means 'is based on' or 'has its foundation in' — the theory rests on the assumption about self-interest.
wrongReasons: 'Leads to' expresses a consequence — the theory does not produce the assumption; the assumption is its starting point.
'Is characterized by' describes a defining feature — while possible, it would mean self-interest is a feature of the theory itself, not its foundation.
'Tends to' expresses a general inclination and requires a following verb — 'the theory tends to the assumption' is ungrammatical.
hebrewExplanation: is grounded in = מבוסס על / שורשיו ב. שימוש: מתאר את הבסיס הרעיוני של תיאוריה, טיעון, או עמדה. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is focused on' — grounded in מתאר בסיס, focused on מתאר נושא מרכזי.

text:
Scientists predict that sea levels ________ rise by up to one meter by the end of the century.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Are likely to' expresses probability — scientists believe there is a significant chance that sea levels will rise by that amount.
wrongReasons: 'In addition to' means 'besides' — it adds information and cannot introduce a predicted outcome as a verb phrase does.
'Apart from' means 'besides' or 'except for' — it introduces an exception, not a probability.
'In accordance with' means 'following rules or standards' — sea levels rising cannot be in accordance with a standard.
hebrewExplanation: is likely to = צפוי ל / עשוי ל. שימוש: מביע הסתברות גבוהה אך לא וודאות. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is certain to' — likely to = אולי, certain to = בוודאות.

text:
The concept of democracy, ________ voting and representation, remains central to modern governance.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'In terms of' frames or defines the concept of democracy through the lens of specific features — voting and representation.
wrongReasons: 'As a result of' expresses causation — democracy is not a result of voting; voting is a component of democracy.
'So as to' expresses purpose — 'democracy so as to voting and representation' is ungrammatical and illogical.
'Owing to' means 'because of' — it introduces a cause; democracy does not exist because of voting and representation in the way this phrase suggests.
hebrewExplanation: in terms of = מבחינת / מבחינה של. שימוש: מגדיר את הזווית או ההיבט שממנו מדובר על נושא. טעות נפוצה: לשכוח להמשיך עם שם עצם — 'in terms of' עוקב תמיד אחריו שם עצם, לא פועל.

text:
The financial crisis of 2008 ________ widespread job losses and a significant drop in consumer confidence.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Led to' expresses a direct causal link — the financial crisis caused or produced widespread job losses and reduced consumer confidence.
wrongReasons: 'Is defined by' describes a defining characteristic — while partly true, 'the crisis is defined by widespread job losses' changes the meaning to a description, not a causal chain.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — the crisis did not ignore job losses; it caused them.
'With respect to' frames a topic — 'the financial crisis with respect to widespread job losses' does not convey causation.
hebrewExplanation: led to = הוביל ל / גרם ל. שימוש: מביע קשר סיבתי ישיר — A גרם ל-B. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'led to cause' — אחרי led to בא שם עצם, לא פועל.

text:
________ social media use, researchers have identified a significant increase in reported anxiety among teenagers.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Due to' expresses causation — social media use is identified as the cause of the increase in anxiety among teenagers.
wrongReasons: 'In contrast to' shows opposition — saying the increase in anxiety is in contrast to social media use contradicts a causal relationship.
'Apart from' means 'besides' or 'except for' — it introduces an exception, not a cause.
'Is likely to' is a verb phrase — it cannot begin a prepositional phrase introducing a cause.
hebrewExplanation: due to = עקב / בגלל / בשל. שימוש: מביע סיבה, נפוץ בכתיבה אקדמית ופורמלית. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'due to the fact that' כשניתן לכתוב פשוט 'because' — הצורה הקצרה עדיפה.

text:
The study examined student performance ________ engagement, motivation, and academic achievement.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'With regard to' introduces the specific aspects or dimensions being examined — engagement, motivation, and achievement are the dimensions of student performance under study.
wrongReasons: 'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — the study examined performance while ignoring these factors, which contradicts the sentence's meaning.
'In order to' expresses purpose — it must be followed by a base verb, not a noun phrase like 'engagement, motivation, and academic achievement'.
'Gives rise to' means 'causes' — the study does not produce engagement; it examines it.
hebrewExplanation: with regard to = בנוגע ל / ביחס ל. שימוש: מציג את הנושא הספציפי שנדון, דומה ל-'regarding' ו-'with respect to'. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'with regards to' (ברבים) — הצורה הפורמלית הנכונה היא 'with regard to' (ביחיד).

text:
Coral reefs are ________ their extraordinary biodiversity and their sensitivity to water temperature changes.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Known for' means recognized or famous for something — coral reefs are widely recognized for their biodiversity and sensitivity.
wrongReasons: 'Likely to' expresses probability and must be followed by a base verb — 'likely to their biodiversity' is ungrammatical.
'Derived from' means 'originating from' — coral reefs do not come from their biodiversity; they possess it.
'Leading to' expresses causation — coral reefs do not cause their own biodiversity in this sentence context.
hebrewExplanation: known for = ידועים בזכות / מפורסמים ב. שימוש: מתאר תכונה בולטת שדרכה מכירים את הנושא. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'known as' — known for עוקב אחריו תכונה, known as עוקב אחריו שם/כינוי.

text:
Stress at work ________ physical symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and difficulty concentrating.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Can give rise to' means 'can cause' or 'can produce' — workplace stress can generate or bring about physical symptoms.
wrongReasons: 'In contrast to' introduces a comparison — 'stress in contrast to physical symptoms' implies they are opposites, which distorts the causal relationship.
'With the aim of' expresses purpose — stress does not have a goal of producing headaches and fatigue.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — workplace stress does not ignore physical symptoms; it causes them.
hebrewExplanation: give rise to = גורם ל / מוליד. שימוש: מתאר שמשהו מצמיח או גורם להופעתו של דבר אחר. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'gives rise for' — הצירוף הנכון הוא 'gives rise to'.

text:
The economic model proposed by the committee ________ assumptions drawn from behavioral economics.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Is derived from' means 'is obtained from' or 'originates in' — the economic model has its source in behavioral economics assumptions.
wrongReasons: 'Plays a role in' describes a contribution — 'the model plays a role in assumptions' reverses the relationship; the assumptions shape the model, not vice versa.
'Is likely to' expresses probability and requires a following verb — 'the model is likely to assumptions' is ungrammatical.
'In spite of' introduces concession — 'the model proposed in spite of assumptions' implies the committee ignored the assumptions, contradicting the meaning.
hebrewExplanation: is derived from = נגזר מ / מקורו ב. שימוש: מתאר שמשהו מקורו או שורשיו בדבר אחר. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is based on' — derived from מדגיש מקור, based on מדגיש בסיס או עיקרון.

text:
The organization revised its guidelines ________ comply with international safety standards.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'So as to' expresses purpose — the organization revised its guidelines with the aim of complying with international standards.
wrongReasons: 'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — this would mean the organization revised guidelines while ignoring safety standards, which contradicts the sentence.
'In terms of' frames a topic — 'revised its guidelines in terms of comply' is ungrammatical; 'comply' is a verb, not a noun phrase.
'Apart from' means 'besides' or 'except for' — it introduces an exception, not a purpose for revising guidelines.
hebrewExplanation: so as to = כדי ל / במטרה ל. שימוש: מביע מטרה, שקול ל-'in order to', אך נפוץ יותר בשלילה (so as not to). טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'so as to complying' — אחרי so as to בא פועל בצורת הבסיס.

text:
The experiment's results ________ support the hypothesis that caffeine improves short-term memory.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Appear to' expresses that something seems to be the case based on available evidence — the results seem to support the hypothesis, though not conclusively.
wrongReasons: 'In accordance with' means 'following rules or standards' — 'the results in accordance with support the hypothesis' is ungrammatical and illogical.
'Is associated with' is a verb phrase for a singular subject — 'results is associated with' is ungrammatical; moreover, it would link results to the hypothesis, not to supporting it.
'With the aim of' expresses purpose — results cannot have an aim; they are data, not intentional actions.
hebrewExplanation: appear to = נראה כי / מסתמן ש. שימוש: מביע ראיה לא מוחלטת, מרכך טענה מדעית. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'seem to' — שניהם כמעט זהים, אך appear to מעט רשמי יותר.

text:
________ recent surveys, job satisfaction among remote workers is significantly higher than among office workers.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'In accordance with' can introduce findings that are consistent with or reported by surveys — the data from surveys show this pattern. Note: 'According to' is the more common choice, but 'in accordance with' is grammatically valid here as it indicates agreement with the survey data.
wrongReasons: 'In terms of' frames a topic — 'in terms of recent surveys, job satisfaction is higher' would mean we are discussing job satisfaction from the perspective of surveys, which is awkward.
'Owing to' means 'because of' — saying job satisfaction is higher because of surveys makes no sense; surveys measure, not cause, satisfaction.
'In spite of' introduces concession — 'in spite of recent surveys, satisfaction is higher' would suggest satisfaction is high against what surveys predicted, which distorts the meaning.
hebrewExplanation: in accordance with = בהתאם ל / בהתאם לנתוני. שימוש: מציג התאמה עם נתונים, כללים, או מקורות. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'according to' — according to פשוט יותר ונפוץ יותר לציטוט מקורות.

text:
Antibiotics should be used responsibly; ________, overuse can lead to the development of drug-resistant bacteria.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Otherwise' connects the warning about responsible use to its consequence — if antibiotics are not used responsibly, the result will be drug resistance.
wrongReasons: 'As opposed to' introduces a contrast — the sentence is not contrasting two things but explaining a condition and its consequence.
'In addition to' means 'besides' — it adds information but does not introduce the conditional consequence of irresponsible antibiotic use.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — it implies overuse happens no matter what, removing the conditional logic of responsible use.
hebrewExplanation: otherwise = אחרת / אם לא כן. שימוש: מציג את התוצאה השלילית אם התנאי הקודם לא יתקיים. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'on the other hand' — otherwise מביע תוצאה מותנית, on the other hand מביע ניגוד.

text:
The new bridge was constructed ________ reducing travel time between the two cities.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'With the aim of' expresses the intended purpose of the construction — the bridge was built with the goal of reducing travel time.
wrongReasons: 'In spite of' introduces concession — 'the bridge was constructed in spite of reducing travel time' implies it was built despite the reduction, which makes no sense.
'As opposed to' introduces contrast — 'the bridge was constructed as opposed to reducing travel time' implies the bridge is the opposite of a time-reducing measure.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — saying the bridge was built regardless of reducing travel time implies the purpose was irrelevant, contradicting the sentence.
hebrewExplanation: with the aim of = במטרה ל / כדי ל. שימוש: מביע כוונה או מטרה, נפוץ בתיאור פרויקטים ומדיניות. טעות נפוצה: לשכוח שאחרי 'with the aim of' בא גרונד (פועל+ing), לא פועל בצורת הבסיס.

text:
The decline in bee populations is ________ widespread use of pesticides, habitat loss, and climate change.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Attributable to' means 'caused by' or 'can be assigned to as a cause' — the decline in bee populations can be attributed to multiple environmental factors.
wrongReasons: 'Regarded as' expresses perception — 'the decline is regarded as widespread pesticide use' incorrectly equates the decline with its causes.
'Characterized by' describes a defining feature — the decline is not characterized by pesticides; rather, pesticides are a cause of the decline.
'Inclined toward' describes a personal tendency — a population decline cannot be inclined toward anything; this phrase applies to people or living beings.
hebrewExplanation: attributable to = מיוחס ל / ניתן לייחס ל. שימוש: מציין שניתן לזהות גורם ספציפי כסיבה לתופעה — נפוץ בניתוח סיבות. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'attributed to' (פעיל) לעומת 'attributable to' (פוטנציאל ייחוס).

text:
The success of the vaccination campaign was ________ the government's commitment to public health education.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'In part due to' means the commitment was one of several contributing causes — the success was partly caused by the government's commitment to health education.
wrongReasons: 'In contrast to' introduces opposition — 'the success was in contrast to the government's commitment' implies the success occurred despite, not because of, that commitment.
'Irrespective of' means 'regardless of' — 'success irrespective of the government's commitment' implies the commitment was irrelevant, contradicting a causal relationship.
'In terms of' frames a perspective — 'the success was in terms of the government's commitment' is grammatically awkward and does not convey causation.
hebrewExplanation: in part due to = בחלקו בשל / בין השאר עקב. שימוש: מציין סיבה חלקית מתוך כמה, מרכך את הקשר הסיבתי. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'in part because of to' — הצורה הנכונה היא 'in part due to' בלבד.

text:
The philosopher's argument ________ the premise that knowledge can only be acquired through direct experience.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Is contingent upon' means 'depends on' or 'is conditional on' — the argument only holds if the premise about direct experience is accepted.
wrongReasons: 'Has given rise to' means 'has caused' — the argument does not produce the premise; the premise is the argument's starting assumption.
'Plays a role in' describes a contribution — 'the argument plays a role in the premise' implies the argument influences the premise, but the premise is actually the foundation of the argument.
'Tends to' expresses habitual inclination and requires a following verb — 'the argument tends to the premise' is ungrammatical.
hebrewExplanation: is contingent upon = מותנה ב / תלוי ב (כתנאי). שימוש: מביע שדבר אחד תלוי בתנאי ספציפי אחר. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is dependent on' — שניהם דומים, אך contingent upon מדגיש תנאיות יותר.

text:
Urban expansion has been found ________ negatively impact biodiversity in surrounding ecosystems.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Found to' (has been found to) is the correct passive construction — urban expansion was discovered to have a negative impact on biodiversity.
wrongReasons: 'In order to' expresses purpose — 'urban expansion has been found in order to impact biodiversity' implies expansion was discovered for the purpose of impacting ecosystems, which is nonsensical.
'So as to' also expresses purpose — the same error applies; urban expansion is not designed to impact ecosystems.
'With the aim of' also expresses purpose followed by a gerund — 'has been found with the aim of negatively impacting' is grammatically wrong and changes the meaning entirely.
hebrewExplanation: has been found to = נמצא כי / התגלה ש (בגוף סביל). שימוש: 'has been found to + verb' — צורת גוף סביל מדעית. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'has been found to negatively impacting' — אחרי 'found to' בא פועל בצורת הבסיס.

text:
The curriculum was restructured ________ a recommendation issued by the national education authority.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'In response to' means 'as a reaction to' — the curriculum was restructured because of, and in reaction to, the authority's recommendation.
wrongReasons: 'As opposed to' introduces contrast — 'restructured as opposed to a recommendation' implies the restructuring is the alternative to following the recommendation, contradicting the sentence.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — this implies the recommendation was ignored, but the sentence suggests the restructuring was triggered by it.
'In addition to' means 'besides' — 'restructured in addition to a recommendation' would mean the restructuring happened alongside the recommendation, which loses the causal connection.
hebrewExplanation: in response to = בתגובה ל / בעקבות. שימוש: מתאר פעולה שנעשתה בתגובה להמלצה, ביקורת, או אירוע. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'as a result of' — in response to מרמז על פעולה מכוונת, as a result of מרמז על תוצאה ישירה.

text:
The research team has demonstrated that exercise ________ improve cognitive function in elderly patients.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Has been shown to' is used when reporting that research has demonstrated a finding — exercise has been proven through studies to improve cognitive function.
wrongReasons: 'Tends to' expresses a habitual pattern, not a demonstrated scientific finding — while grammatically possible, it is weaker and does not carry the sense of proven research.
'Is characterized by' describes a defining feature — exercise does not have cognitive function as a feature; rather, exercise causes improvements in cognitive function.
'Is grounded in' means 'is based on' — exercise is not based on improving cognitive function; it causes it.
hebrewExplanation: has been shown to = הוכח כי / נמצא ש (בגוף סביל). שימוש: מציג ממצא מחקרי מוכח, חזק יותר מ-'tends to'. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'has been shown that exercise' — הצורה הנכונה היא 'exercise has been shown to improve'.

text:
The university's admissions policy is ________ academic merit, with particular emphasis on mathematics and science scores.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Primarily based on' means the policy's main foundation is academic merit — it is derived from and built upon this criterion above all others.
wrongReasons: 'Associated with' expresses a general connection or correlation — 'the admissions policy is associated with academic merit' is vague and implies only a link, not that merit is the foundation.
'Likely to' expresses probability and requires a following verb — 'the policy is likely to academic merit' is ungrammatical.
'In contrast to' introduces opposition — saying the policy is in contrast to academic merit implies it opposes merit-based admissions, which contradicts the sentence.
hebrewExplanation: primarily based on = מבוסס בעיקרו על. שימוש: מדגיש שהבסיס העיקרי הוא קריטריון ספציפי מתוך כמה. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'mainly based in' — הצירוף הנכון הוא 'based on', לא 'based in'.

text:
The author's conclusions ________ a broad reading of historical evidence from multiple civilizations.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Are informed by' means 'are shaped by' or 'are based on' — the conclusions draw from and are influenced by the historical evidence.
wrongReasons: 'Give rise to' means 'produce' or 'cause' — if the conclusions give rise to historical evidence, that means they create it, which reverses the logical relationship.
'Are inclined to' describes a tendency of a person — conclusions cannot be inclined toward anything; this phrase is used for people or living things.
'Play a key role in' describes a contribution — the conclusions do not significantly contribute to the historical evidence; rather, the evidence informs the conclusions.
hebrewExplanation: are informed by = מושפעים מ / מעוצבים על ידי. שימוש: מתאר השפעה אינטלקטואלית על טיעון, מחקר, או עמדה. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'are based on' — informed by מדגיש השפעה, based on מדגיש תמיכה.

text:
The report calls for a fundamental restructuring of the tax system, ________ eliminating loopholes that disproportionately benefit the wealthy.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Most notably by' specifies the most important method or example of the restructuring — eliminating loopholes is the primary means being called for.
wrongReasons: 'In spite of' introduces concession — 'calls for restructuring in spite of eliminating loopholes' would suggest restructuring is sought despite the loophole elimination, which contradicts the meaning.
'Regardless of' means 'without being affected by' — 'calls for restructuring regardless of eliminating loopholes' implies the call ignores loopholes, which is the opposite of the intended meaning.

'As a result of' expresses causation — 'calls for restructuring as a result of eliminating loopholes' implies that existing loophole elimination already caused this call, which distorts the sentence's future-oriented meaning.
hebrewExplanation: most notably = ובעיקר / ובולט מכולם. שימוש: מציין את הדוגמה הבולטת ביותר מתוך כמה. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'most importantly' — most notably מציין את הדוגמה הבולטת, most importantly מציין את הדבר החשוב ביותר.

text:
Chronic stress has been implicated ________ the onset of a range of autoimmune disorders.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Implicated in' is the correct collocation — 'has been implicated in' means has been identified as a contributing factor to something negative, in this case the onset of autoimmune disorders.
wrongReasons: 'As a result of' expresses causation but cannot replace the preposition 'in' within the phrase 'implicated in' — 'implicated as a result of the onset' reverses the relationship entirely.
'For' changes the meaning entirely — 'implicated for the onset' is ungrammatical; the correct preposition in this collocation is always 'in'.
'With regard to' frames a topic — 'implicated with regard to the onset' does not convey the idea of being identified as a causal factor.
hebrewExplanation: has been implicated in = מעורב ב / נמצא כגורם ל. שימוש: מתאר שמשהו זוהה כגורם חשוד, נפוץ בספרות רפואית ואפידמיולוגית. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'implicated for' — הצירוף הנכון הוא 'implicated in'.

text:
The committee's decision to postpone the vote was widely ________ a strategic maneuver to avoid controversy.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Seen as' means 'perceived as' or 'interpreted as' — the public and commentators perceived the postponement as a deliberate political strategy.
wrongReasons: 'Associated to' is grammatically incorrect — the correct collocation is 'associated with', not 'associated to'.
'Given rise to' means 'caused' or 'produced' — 'the decision was given rise to a maneuver' is grammatically and logically incorrect.
'Linked with' expresses a connection between two things — 'the decision was linked with a strategic maneuver' suggests a correlation, not a perception or interpretation.
hebrewExplanation: seen as = נתפס כ / נחשב ל. שימוש: מביע כיצד הציבור או אחרים תופסים דבר מה. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'regarded as' — שניהם דומים, אך 'seen as' נפוץ יותר בהקשרים פוליטיים וחברתיים.

text:
The two countries reached an agreement that was ________ principles of mutual respect and non-interference.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Grounded in' means 'based on' or 'rooted in' — the agreement was built upon and rested on the foundational principles of mutual respect and non-interference.
wrongReasons: 'Characterized by' describes a defining feature — while the agreement could be characterized by these principles, 'grounded in' better expresses that the principles are the foundation, not just a feature.
'Leading to' expresses causation — 'an agreement leading to principles' implies the agreement produced the principles, which reverses the logical direction.
'Likely to' expresses probability and requires a following verb — 'agreement likely to principles of mutual respect' is ungrammatical.
hebrewExplanation: grounded in = מבוסס על / שורשיו ב. שימוש: מתאר בסיס רעיוני עמוק יותר מ-'based on' — מרמז על שורשים אידיאולוגיים או ערכיים. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'grounded on' — הצירוף הנכון הוא 'grounded in'.

text:
The sharp drop in consumer spending is ________ growing uncertainty in the housing market.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'In part attributable to' means one contributing cause of the drop is housing market uncertainty — 'in part' signals there are multiple causes and this is one of them.
wrongReasons: 'Likely to' expresses probability and requires a following verb — 'the drop is likely to growing uncertainty' is ungrammatical and illogical.
'Characterized by' describes a defining feature — the spending drop is not characterized by uncertainty; uncertainty is a cause of the drop, not a feature of it.
'In accordance with' means 'consistent with rules or standards' — consumer spending does not follow rules of market uncertainty; it responds to it.
hebrewExplanation: in part attributable to = ניתן לייחס אותו בחלקו ל. שימוש: מציין אחת מכמה סיבות לתופעה — מרכך את הקשר הסיבתי. טעות נפוצה: לכתוב 'in part attributed to' — attributable to (תואר) נכון בהקשר זה.

text:
The algorithm's performance ________ the quality and volume of data it is trained on.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Is largely contingent on' means the performance depends heavily on the quality and volume of training data — it is conditional on those factors.
wrongReasons: 'Has been shown to' reports a scientific finding and requires a following verb — 'performance has been shown to the quality of data' is ungrammatical.
'Plays a role in' describes a contribution — 'the algorithm's performance plays a role in the quality of data' reverses the relationship: data quality affects performance, not the other way around.
'Gives rise to' means 'causes' — 'the algorithm's performance gives rise to the quality of data' implies performance creates data quality, which is the opposite of what the sentence means.
hebrewExplanation: is contingent on = מותנה ב / תלוי ב. שימוש: מביע תלות תנאית — הביצועים תלויים ישירות בנתונים. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'is dependent on' — contingent on מדגיש תנאיות, dependent on מדגיש תלות כללית.

text:
The research challenges prior assumptions ________ the relationship between income inequality and social mobility.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Pertaining to' means 'relating to' or 'concerning' — the prior assumptions are about the relationship between income inequality and social mobility.
wrongReasons: 'Due to' means 'because of' — 'assumptions due to the relationship' implies the relationship caused the assumptions; the sentence says the assumptions are about the relationship.
'Giving rise to' means 'causing' — 'assumptions giving rise to the relationship' implies assumptions produce the relationship between inequality and mobility, which distorts the meaning.
'In spite of' introduces concession — 'challenges assumptions in spite of the relationship' implies research ignores the relationship, which does not match the intended meaning.
hebrewExplanation: pertaining to = הנוגע ל / הקשור ל. שימוש: מתאר שמשהו שייך לנושא או קשור אליו, נפוץ בכתיבה משפטית ואקדמית. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'related to' — pertaining to רשמי יותר ומדויק יותר בהקשרים אקדמיים.

text:
The study's methodology was ________ criticism due to its small sample size and lack of control group.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Subject to' means 'open to' or 'exposed to' — the methodology was open to or vulnerable to criticism because of its limitations.
wrongReasons: 'In accordance with' means 'consistent with' or 'following rules' — 'the methodology was in accordance with criticism' implies it was designed to match criticism, which is logically absurd.
'Grounded in' means 'based on' — 'the methodology was grounded in criticism' would mean the methodology was built on criticism, not that it received criticism.
'Apart from' means 'besides' or 'except for' — 'the methodology was apart from criticism' implies it was separate from or not subject to criticism, which contradicts the sentence.
hebrewExplanation: subject to = חשוף ל / פתוח ל. שימוש: מתאר שמשהו עשוי לקבל ביקורת, שינוי, או השפעה — נפוץ בכתיבה אקדמית ומשפטית. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'subjected to' — subject to = חשוף ל (תואר), subjected to = נחשף ל (פועל עבר).

text:
The phenomenon of urbanization is ________ a complex interplay of economic, demographic, and environmental factors.
Choose the academic phrase that best completes the sentence.
explanation: 'Underpinned by' means 'supported by' or 'driven by underlying factors' — urbanization is sustained and driven by the complex interaction of these factors.
wrongReasons: 'Inclined to' describes a personal tendency and must be followed by a base verb — 'urbanization is inclined to a complex interplay' is grammatically wrong and applies only to living beings.
'Likely to' expresses probability and requires a following verb — 'urbanization is likely to a complex interplay' is ungrammatical.

'Derived to' is not a valid English phrase — the correct collocation is 'derived from', and even then it would not fit this context since urbanization is not derived from these factors; it is driven by them.
hebrewExplanation: underpinned by = מבוסס על / נתמך על ידי. שימוש: מתאר את הכוחות הבסיסיים שמניעים תופעה — נפוץ בניתוח מדיניות וכלכלה. טעות נפוצה: לבלבל עם 'supported by' — underpinned by מדגיש מבנה תמיכה עמוק יותר.
